slos.sli_definition: 99th percentile request latency at or below target

headroom.margin_rationale: 1 stddev (95ms) headroom above observed p99 for growing maturity tier

slos.rationale: The observed p99 latency is 500.0ms with a stddev of 95.0ms and sample count of 500000. The p50-to-p99 ratio of 4.2x (120ms to 500ms) indicates a meaningful latency tail. payment-gateway contributes 67% of p99 tail latency (320ms of 480ms service p99). Latency SLO headroom should account for dependency variance. Setting the SLA target at 595ms provides 1 stddev of headroom above observed p99, which accommodates normal variance without false alerts. The 95ms stddev represents 19% of the p99 value, suggesting moderate variability. Focus improvement efforts on payment-gateway as the dominant latency contributor before tightening below 500ms.

slos.sli_definition: Ratio of successful requests at or above target

headroom.margin_rationale: 2 stddev (0.0006 each) below observed availability for growing maturity tier

slos.rationale: The observed availability is 0.998 (99.8%) based on 1000 errors out of 500000 total requests, with error rate stddev of 0.0006. connection_timeout errors account for 58.6% of all failures. Addressing upstream connectivity would have the highest impact on improving availability. Setting the SLA target at 0.9968 provides 2 stddev of headroom below observed availability, which absorbs normal error rate fluctuation and minor incident windows. The low error rate stddev of 0.0006 (30% of the error rate) indicates relatively stable error behavior. This target is achievable without infrastructure changes based on the current 7d baseline.
